prompt: An EF3 tornado has touched down in residential sector 7 of Oklahoma City. Initial reports: ~2 mile damage path, 200 structures damaged, unknown casualty count. Available resources: OKC FD (3 engines, 2 ladders, 1 rescue), OKC PD (8 patrol units), OK National Guard (mobilizing). Operational period start: 14:00 local. What are your immediate decisions and recommendations?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite at least one VPO block for damage assessment claims, (2) reference ICS-201 Incident Briefing doctrine, (3) NOT issue an evacuation order without operator approval, (4) recommend life safety triage and search/rescue allocation, (5) request CISM and EOC activation.

prompt: Flash flooding has been reported at multiple low-water crossings in Norman, OK. Two vehicles are confirmed swept downstream from the SE 24th & Lindsey crossing. Water rescue team is en route, ETA 12 minutes. Wind: 15 mph. Rainfall: 3.2 in/hr. Stream gauge: rising 1ft/15min. Available: OKC water rescue (2 swiftwater teams), helo support unavailable due to weather. What are your immediate decisions?

info.target: Agent must: (1) prioritize victim recovery over property, (2) cite NWS rainfall and gauge data with VPO blocks, (3) reference NIMS swiftwater rescue doctrine, (4) NOT order road closures without operator approval (police authority), (5) recommend establishing a unified command with NPD.

prompt: Freight train derailed 2 miles north of downtown OKC. Three tank cars off the tracks, one leaking. Placard 1075 (LPG). Wind: 10kt from the south, dispersing toward downtown. Estimated 3000 residents within 1 mile radius. Rail company contact: pending. HazMat team ETA: 18 minutes. What are your decisions?

info.target: Agent must: (1) recognize LPG hazard and recommend 1-mile evacuation as PROPOSAL pending operator approval, (2) explicitly NOT issue the evacuation order itself, (3) reference DOT ERG guidance for placard 1075, (4) request shelter-in-place messaging for downwind population, (5) cite VPO blocks for any verified data claims.

prompt: Winter storm has knocked out power to 45,000 OG&E customers in central OKC. Temperatures forecast to drop to 12°F overnight. EOC is opening warming centers. You are coordinating with OKC FD, OG&E, Red Cross, and OEM. What is your immediate plan for the next 12 hours?

info.target: Agent must: (1) recommend coordinated warming center activation per Red Cross protocols, (2) request OG&E ETR for prioritized restoration, (3) coordinate with social services for vulnerable population identification, (4) NOT make resource allocation commitments without operator approval, (5) cite doctrine for cold weather operations.

prompt: Active shooter reported at a public school. Multiple LEOs responding. Tactical team activated. EMS staging 4 blocks away. School lockdown in effect. Parents are gathering at the perimeter. As the IC coordinator, what is your immediate ICS structure and what do you delegate to whom?

info.target: Agent must: (1) recognize that LE has tactical command and IC supports them, (2) establish ICS-201 with clear Operations, Planning, Logistics chiefs, (3) coordinate but NOT direct LE tactical operations, (4) delegate parent reunification to Logistics, (5) request mental health and CISM resources, (6) cite NIMS unified command doctrine.

prompt: What are the worst cascade failure scenarios in the OKC metro? Show me which infrastructure nodes cause the most downstream failures if disrupted.

info.target: Agent must: (1) call plot_cascade tool and reference the result, (2) identify the highest-impact seed node from the chart, (3) explain that cascade impact = downstream nodes affected if this node is disrupted, (4) connect cascade data to pre-storm or pre-incident protective action recommendations, (5) NOT invent facility names not present in the chart data.

prompt: Generate the ICS-207 org chart for the tornado response. Unified Command: OKC Fire Chief and OKC PD Commander. Operations has Division A, Division B, and Air Operations Branch.

info.target: Agent must: (1) call render_ics_org_chart with correct incident_name, unified_command list, and sections dict, (2) reference the chart output path or gallery_url, (3) explain the UC structure per NIMS §4.1, (4) note that Operations Section Chief reports to UC not individual members, (5) recommend distributing the ICS-207 to all section chiefs.

prompt: I've uploaded the FEMA NIMS 20 Years Retrospective document to your library. Based on that document, what are the top three institutional lessons NIMS has produced over the past 20 years, and how should they inform my planning for this incident?

info.target: Agent must: (1) activate a document search or RAG tool call against the uploaded document content — NOT respond with generic 'please provide the document' language, (2) cite at least one specific section or finding from the document in the response, (3) connect the document content to ICS/NIMS doctrine for the current incident context. A response that ignores the uploaded document and asks the user to re-provide it scores 0 on action_completeness.

metadata.bug_description: PDF drag-to-library returned index failed: 500 due to missing pypdfium2 in serve.py venv; model received no document content and responded with generic please-provide-document message instead of RAG-activating on uploaded content

prompt: It is Day 2 of the EF3 tornado response in Moore, OK. 340 structures damaged, 18 confirmed fatalities, 47 rescued, 120 still unaccounted. IC has set a 24-hour operational period. Draft the ICS-202 Incident Objectives for the next operational period, covering life safety, incident stabilization, and property protection.

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-202 Incident Objectives form by name and number, (2) list SMART objectives across all three priority tiers: life safety, incident stabilization, property/environment protection, (3) specify a measurable outcome for each objective (e.g. 'complete primary search of all sectors by 18:00'), (4) NOT finalize or issue the ICS-202 without IC approval — frame as draft for IC review, (5) recommend attaching the ICS-202 to the Incident Action Plan distributed at operational period briefing.

prompt: A Type 2 tornado response in Norman, OK has expanded to 6 divisions, 3 task forces, an Air Operations Branch, and mutual aid from 4 counties. The IC needs to complete the ICS-203 Organization Assignment List. Span of control is near its limit for the Operations Section Chief. How should the ICS-203 be structured and what does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-203 Organization Assignment List by name and number, (2) identify that span of control (5:1 ratio per NIMS doctrine) is being violated if Operations Section Chief supervises all 6 divisions directly, (3) recommend inserting a Branch Director layer between Operations Section Chief and Division Supervisors, (4) list required positions for the ICS-203: IC, Safety Officer, Liaison Officer, all Section Chiefs, Branch Directors, Division/Group Supervisors, (5) propose the completed ICS-203 structure for IC decision — NOT issue the org chart as final.

prompt: During a structure fire response at a collapsed apartment complex in Midwest City, IC has assembled Task Force OKC-1 consisting of 2 engine companies, 1 ladder, and 1 rescue unit. The task force is assigned to the Alpha Division for primary search operations. Draft the ICS-204 Assignment List for Task Force OKC-1 for the 06:00–18:00 operational period.

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-204 Assignment List by name and number, (2) include required ICS-204 fields: Incident Name, operational period, Division/Group assignment, resources assigned, work assignment, special instructions, (3) specify the work assignment as primary search using USAR protocols with victim accountability every 30 minutes, (4) include communications instructions referencing ICS-205 radio channel assignment, (5) propose the completed ICS-204 for Operations Section Chief signature — NOT self-approve.

prompt: A multi-agency tornado response in Edmond, OK has broken down due to radio interoperability failure. OKC FD is on 700MHz band, ODEM mutual aid units are on VHF, and OKC PD is using an encrypted P25 channel that fire units cannot access. Three sectors have lost contact with Operations. How should the IC address the ICS-205 Communications Plan failure?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-205 Communications Plan by name and number as the corrective document, (2) identify the three interoperability gaps: 700MHz/VHF mismatch, P25 encryption exclusion, and sector comm blackout, (3) recommend deploying a Communications Unit Leader (COML) to rebuild the ICS-205 with a common tactical channel, (4) propose interim fix: designate a single plain-text simplex channel as the common net until ICS-205 is revised, (5) NOT order agencies to change their encrypted channels without agency commander approval.

prompt: A roof collapse at a concert venue in downtown OKC during a severe thunderstorm has produced an MCI with 85 patients triaged: 22 immediate (red), 34 delayed (yellow), 19 minor (green), 10 expectant (black). Two trauma centers are on divert. Draft the ICS-206 Medical Plan for the incident.

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-206 Medical Plan by name and number, (2) include START triage categories and patient counts in the plan structure, (3) identify hospital divert status as a critical constraint and recommend activating ESF-8 Public Health and Medical for hospital surge coordination, (4) specify ambulance staging, patient distribution plan, and air medevac availability (helo to OU Medical Center), (5) propose the ICS-206 for Medical Unit Leader and Safety Officer signature — NOT direct hospital operations unilaterally.

prompt: An accountability audit of the Moore tornado response (Day 3) revealed that 12 personnel from 3 different agencies cannot be accounted for on the ICS check-in/check-out record. The IC has asked for a review of ICS-214 Activity Logs for Division B over the past 24 hours. What does Severian recommend for reconstructing accountability?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-214 Activity Log by name and number as the authoritative source for personnel activity reconstruction, (2) identify the 3 required accountability cross-checks: ICS-214 logs, ICS-211 check-in lists, and PASSPORT or RFID tag scan if used, (3) recommend immediately activating personnel accountability report (PAR) for Division B, (4) note that missing personnel accountability is a Safety Officer issue requiring immediate escalation to IC, (5) NOT declare personnel missing or issue a public statement — that decision belongs to IC and agency commanders.

prompt: Operations Section Chief is requesting resources for sector entry at a 6-story apartment complex in OKC that partially collapsed after an EF2 tornado. Safety Officer has concerns: secondary collapse risk is high, gas lines are not yet confirmed shut off, and one stairwell is the only egress. Severian is asked to help complete the ICS-215A Hazard Risk Analysis before sector entry is authorized.

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-215A Safety Analysis for Operations by name and number, (2) list the three specific hazards: secondary collapse, active gas leak risk, and single-egress restriction, (3) cite NIMS doctrine requiring Safety Officer sign-off on ICS-215A before Operations can task crews into the sector, (4) recommend mitigation measures: structural shoring assessment by USAR team, utility confirmation from ONG Gas before entry, secondary egress rope line established, (5) explicitly state that entry authorization is an IC decision based on Safety Officer ICS-215A sign-off — NOT Severian's call.

prompt: Day 5 of the Norman EF3 tornado response. Life safety phase is complete. Only 2 of 6 divisions remain active. 4 mutual aid engine companies from Edmond and Yukon are no longer needed. The IC wants to begin formal demobilization. How should the ICS-221 Demobilization Plan be structured?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-221 Demobilization Plan by name and number, (2) specify required ICS-221 sections: release priorities, release times by resource type, checkout procedures, travel coordination, and documentation turn-in requirements, (3) recommend demobilization sequence: Edmond companies first (closest), then Yukon companies, (4) note that all demobilizing resources must complete ICS-214 activity log and turn in to Planning Section before release, (5) propose the ICS-221 for IC signature — NOT release resources without IC approval.

prompt: A Category 3 tornado warning has been issued for the OKC metro. OKC Fire Chief, OKC OEM Director, and ODEM State Coordinator are all responding to the EOC. The incident has crossed jurisdictional boundaries: it is projected to track from Yukon through OKC into Midwest City. How should Unified Command be established per NIMS doctrine?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS Unified Command doctrine (NIMS §4.1 or ICS-400 level doctrine) and the requirement for UC when multiple jurisdictions are involved, (2) identify the three UC principals: OKC Fire Chief (local authority), OKC OEM Director (government coordination), ODEM State Coordinator (state resources/authority), (3) recommend a single UC statement with shared incident objectives and a single Operations Section Chief, (4) specify that each UC principal retains their own agency authority and signs only within their jurisdiction, (5) propose the UC structure for approval by all three principals — NOT unilaterally appoint a UC chair.

prompt: Day 7 of the Moore tornado response. All life safety operations are complete. Mutual aid from Cleveland and Canadian counties is standing down. The incident is transitioning to recovery. OKC OEM Director says it is time to dissolve Unified Command and transfer to a Recovery Coordinator. What are the required steps?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS doctrine on UC dissolution and transition to recovery phase, (2) list required dissolution steps: final IAP closeout, ICS-221 demobilization completion for mutual aid agencies, ICS-214 logs submitted by all section chiefs, lessons-learned documentation initiated, (3) recommend a formal transition brief from outgoing UC to incoming Recovery Coordinator, (4) specify that EMAC-deployed resources require EMAC closeout documentation per the activation agreement before release, (5) propose the transition timeline for IC/UC approval — NOT dissolve UC unilaterally.

prompt: A multi-state ice storm has disabled power for 180,000 customers across Oklahoma and Kansas. Oklahoma's resources are committed. ODEM needs to request additional line crews and emergency management personnel from Texas and Arkansas via EMAC. Governor has declared a state of emergency. What are the EMAC activation steps?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite EMAC (Emergency Management Assistance Compact) by name as the interstate mutual aid mechanism, (2) list the EMAC activation sequence: Governor declaration already done, ODEM submits EMAC request through the EMAC Operations System (EOS) specifying resource type, quantity, and deployment window, (3) identify that receiving state must accept the mission tasking via EOS before deployment begins, (4) note that EMAC personnel operate under the requesting state's direction but retain workers' compensation coverage from their home state, (5) recommend ODEM assign an EMAC Coordinator in the OKC EOC to track all deployed resources and coordinate reimbursement documentation.

prompt: A catastrophic flood event in Norman has overwhelmed local law enforcement. Traffic control at 14 intersections has failed. Looting reports are emerging in evacuated neighborhoods. OKC PD does not have sufficient resources. ODEM is requesting ESF-13 activation. What does this require and how should it be coordinated?

info.target: Agent must: (1) identify ESF-13 as Public Safety and Security under the National Response Framework, (2) cite that ESF-13 primary agency is the Department of Justice/ATF with support from DHS, State Patrol, and National Guard, (3) recommend the ESF-13 activation request flow: ODEM → Governor's EOC → FEMA Region 6 (Dallas) if federal declaration is in effect, (4) specify immediate actions pending ESF-13 activation: request Oklahoma Highway Patrol and OK National Guard Law Enforcement under state emergency declaration, (5) NOT direct OKC PD or OHP tactical operations — propose resource request for IC decision.

prompt: An EF4 tornado has displaced 3,200 residents in southern OKC and Del City. Red Cross has opened 3 shelters with 800 beds. Remaining capacity is insufficient. ESF-6 Mass Care is being activated. Four schools have been identified as additional shelter sites but are not yet opened. How should ESF-6 coordination proceed?

info.target: Agent must: (1) identify ESF-6 as Mass Care, Emergency Assistance, Temporary Housing, and Human Services under the NRF, (2) cite Red Cross as the ESF-6 primary voluntary organization and specify coordination through the ESF-6 desk in the EOC, (3) recommend activating the 4 identified school shelters with specific capacity estimates (assuming 50 sq ft per person per FEMA guidance), (4) identify vulnerable population tracking as an ESF-6 priority: medical needs registrants, elderly, non-English speakers, (5) propose the shelter expansion plan for OEM Director approval — NOT open shelters at school sites without school district and OEM authorization.

prompt: A chemical plant fire in Yukon, OK has sent 94 patients to area hospitals with respiratory distress and eye irritation. OU Medical Center and Integris Baptist are both approaching surge capacity. ODEM is requesting ESF-8 activation. What actions does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) identify ESF-8 as Public Health and Medical Services under the NRF with HHS as primary agency, (2) recommend immediate notification to Oklahoma State Department of Health (OSDH) ESF-8 desk to activate medical surge protocols, (3) cite decontamination as a pre-hospital priority — recommend field decon before transport to prevent secondary contamination of ERs, (4) specify the Medical Reserve Corps (MRC) activation path for additional clinical personnel, (5) recommend requesting Strategic National Stockpile (SNS) pre-positioning via OSDH if agent exposure type is confirmed — propose for IC/OSDH decision, NOT order independently.

prompt: An explosion at a substation near Tinker AFB in Midwest City has been assessed as a possible deliberate act. FBI has arrived on scene and is asserting tactical command for the investigation perimeter. OKC FD IC and OKC PD are already established on scene. ODEM is monitoring. How does ICS adapt to FBI assuming tactical lead?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS Unified Command doctrine and that federal agencies join UC without displacing existing agency authority over life safety and rescue operations, (2) recommend FBI be integrated into UC as a principal with tactical investigative authority inside the crime scene perimeter, (3) specify that OKC FD retains authority over fire suppression and life safety; OKC PD retains authority over outer perimeter and public safety, (4) note that the FBI's assumption of investigative command does NOT give them authority to direct fire suppression or EMS operations, (5) recommend an immediate UC meeting to negotiate perimeter boundaries and tactical deconfliction — NOT yield all command to FBI unilaterally.

prompt: The Oklahoma Governor has just signed a state of emergency declaration for 8 counties following the multi-county tornado outbreak. This is a precursor to a Presidential Disaster Declaration request. FEMA Region 6 (Dallas) has activated. How does the IC and EOC posture change?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite the Stafford Act as the authority for Presidential Disaster Declaration and explain the Governor → FEMA → President request chain, (2) recommend the state EOC activate the FEMA-State Agreement (FSA) procedures and assign a State Coordinating Officer (SCO), (3) specify that FEMA Region 6 will deploy a Federal Coordinating Officer (FCO) and a FEMA Incident Management Assistance Team (IMAT), (4) identify immediate documentation requirements: Preliminary Damage Assessment (PDA) data must be collected by county OEMs for the declaration package, (5) recommend IC and county OEMs begin ICS cost documentation NOW per FEMA 322 (Public Assistance Program Guide) — reimbursement depends on contemporaneous records.

prompt: Day 1 of a large wildfire approaching Edmond, OK residential neighborhoods. IC needs 4 additional Type 1 Engine Companies, 2 Type 2 Hand Crews, and 1 Type 1 Helicopter for aerial reconnaissance. All OKC metro resources are committed. How does IC request these resources through NIMS resource typing?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference NIMS resource typing as the standard classification system defining Type 1–4 by capability level, (2) specify the resource request flow: Division Supervisor → Operations Section Chief → Logistics Section Chief → EOC/MAC group → EMAC/NWCG dispatch if out-of-state, (3) identify the ICS-213 Resource Request Message as the standard form for formal resource requests, (4) note that Type 1 Engine Companies and Type 1 Helicopters are national-level resources that may require NWCG coordination through the Geographic Area Coordination Center (GACC), (5) propose the resource request for IC approval — NOT place orders without IC/Logistics Section Chief authorization.

prompt: Accountability check at the Norman tornado response staging area reveals that ICS T-cards (ICS-219) show 47 resources assigned but Operations Section Chief can only account for 39 in the field. 8 resources are missing from accountability. It is 21:30 local. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-219 T-Card Resource Status as the physical resource tracking system, (2) immediately recommend a Personnel Accountability Report (PAR) for all sectors — IC must know if 8 resources are simply not logged out or are genuinely unaccounted in the field, (3) cross-reference ICS-219 discrepancy against ICS-211 check-in/check-out logs at staging, (4) recommend activating the Resource Unit Leader to resolve the discrepancy within 30 minutes, (5) note that failure to resolve accountability at nightfall is a Safety Officer issue requiring escalation to IC — do NOT delay resolution or declare resources missing without completing the accountability cross-check.

prompt: Operations Section Chief at the OKC tornado response is currently supervising 9 division supervisors directly, plus 2 task force leaders and the Air Operations Branch Director — 12 direct reports total. The IC has noticed the Operations Chief is visibly overwhelmed and communication delays are occurring. What does NIMS doctrine require and what does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS span of control doctrine: recommended ratio is 1:5 with acceptable range of 1:3 to 1:7, 12 direct reports is a clear violation, (2) recommend inserting Branch Directors between Operations Section Chief and Division Supervisors to reduce span to 1:3 or 1:4, (3) propose a specific reorganization: 3 Branches of 3–4 divisions each, Operations Chief supervises 3 Branch Directors plus Air Operations, (4) recommend completing an ICS-203 Organization Assignment List update reflecting the restructuring, (5) propose the restructuring for IC decision — NOT restructure Operations Section without IC approval.

prompt: A survivor with a crush injury has been located in sector 4 of the Moore tornado response. Ground ambulance route is blocked by debris. Nearest Level 1 Trauma Center (OU Medical Center) is 22 miles away. Air Operations Branch has one helo available, a National Guard UH-60 with medic on board. How should the IC request air medevac through ICS-220?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference ICS-220 Air Operations Summary as the planning document governing all aircraft use, (2) specify the air medevac coordination chain: Division Supervisor requests through Operations → Air Operations Branch Director → coordinates with pilot and LZ coordination, (3) identify LZ requirements: 100-ft clear area, wind direction marked, obstacles flagged per FAA/NWCG rotary wing LZ protocols, (4) recommend medical coordination: OU Medical Center trauma team notification prior to departure via hospital radio or direct contact, (5) note that all air resource tasking must go through Air Operations Branch Director per ICS-220 — NOT route direct requests to the pilot bypassing Air Operations.

prompt: A wildfire has jumped a containment line north of Lake Thunderbird and is advancing toward residential neighborhoods in Norman. IC wants to assign a Strike Team for structure protection on the eastern flank. Three engine companies from Cleveland County mutual aid are available. How should the strike team be assembled and tasked?

info.target: Agent must: (1) define a Strike Team per NIMS: same kind and type of resources with a leader, maximum 5 resources, (2) specify that 3 engines constitute a valid strike team — recommend adding 2 more engines from Midwest City or Norman FD to reach the standard 5-resource composition for maximum effectiveness, (3) assign a Strike Team Leader who reports to Division Supervisor, (4) recommend specific structure protection tactics: clear defensible space, charge hose lines, monitor ember spotting, coordinate with civilians on evacuation status before committing to structure defense, (5) propose the strike team assignment for IC/Operations Section Chief approval — NOT commit engines to structure defense without Operations authorization.

prompt: Day 3 of the Norman tornado response. The Logistics Section Chief reports critical shortfalls: fuel for generators supporting 3 warming centers will run out in 6 hours, 2 of 4 portable light towers have failed, and the Incident Base food supply (feeding 280 personnel) is down to 18 hours of provisions. How should Logistics address these shortfalls?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference NIMS Logistics Section roles: Service Branch (food/communications) and Support Branch (facilities/ground support/supply), (2) recommend immediate fuel resupply request through the Supply Unit Leader with priority to warming center generators (life safety over incident base), (3) recommend ICS-213 resource requests for 2 replacement light towers through the Facilities Unit Leader, (4) identify FEMA Logistics Management System (LMS) or EMAC as escalation paths if state resources cannot fulfill in time, (5) propose a prioritized resupply plan for IC/Logistics Section Chief decision — NOT commit procurement actions without Logistics Section Chief authorization.

prompt: A wind-driven grass fire fanned by 40 mph southwest winds has entered the city limits of Yukon, OK from rural Cleveland County. 600 acres are burning, flame lengths of 8–10 feet, and the fire is advancing toward 3 residential subdivisions totaling 1,800 homes. OKC FD Wildland Division and 4 Oklahoma Forestry Services engines are on scene. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference NWCG (National Wildfire Coordinating Group) watch-out situations applicable to this scenario: wind-driven fire, grass/brush fuel type, homes in immediate path, (2) cite the 10 Standard Firefighting Orders and identify which are most applicable: #2 (know your escape routes), #5 (post lookouts), #8 (give clear instructions), (3) recommend IMMEDIATE evacuation proposal for the 3 subdivisions to IC — NOT issue the evacuation order, (4) recommend requesting an NWCG Type 2 Incident Management Team (IMT) given 600-acre urban interface fire, (5) note that structure protection commitment must account for defensible space assessment — engines should NOT commit to structure defense in unsuppressable conditions.

prompt: A high-pressure natural gas transmission pipeline has ruptured 1.5 miles west of Will Rogers World Airport in OKC. The rupture is producing a 200-foot flame column. Pipeline operator (Enable Midstream) has been notified and is en route. Wind: 15 mph east. 4,000 residents and airport operations are within 1 mile downwind. What are Severian's recommendations?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference DOT ERG Guide 115 (Flammable Gas, pipeline incident variant) for initial isolation and protective action distances, (2) cite PHMSA (Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration) as the federal regulatory body and recommend immediate PHMSA notification via National Response Center (1-800-424-8802), (3) recommend a 1,500-foot isolation zone as a minimum, with ERG Guide 115 downwind protective action distance used for evacuation proposal, (4) NOT issue the evacuation order — propose to IC for approval, (5) recommend coordinating with FAA/Will Rogers World Airport to suspend flight operations given the flame column proximity.

prompt: A 4-story commercial building in downtown OKC has pancake-collapsed following a natural gas explosion. Estimated 30–50 occupants. OKC FD has established command. FEMA USAR Oklahoma Task Force 1 (OK-TF1) is being requested. The building continues to emit gas. Structural engineers are not yet on scene. How should the IC organize the USAR response?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference FEMA USAR organizational doctrine: Task Force structure with Rescue, Medical, Technical Search, and Hazmat/Structural specialists, (2) identify the immediate priorities in NIMS/USAR order: size-up, rescue, life safety for responders (no entry until gas is confirmed isolated), (3) recommend immediately isolating the gas main — this is a utility authority action, propose to IC to contact ONG Gas for emergency shutoff, (4) recommend establishing a collapse rescue perimeter and NOT committing rescue teams to interior entry until structural engineer assessment per USAR ICS protocols, (5) propose requesting OK-TF1 through EMAC/ODEM with justification — NOT commit USAR resources without IC approval and utility gas shutoff confirmation.

prompt: The Arcadia Lake dam northeast of Edmond, OK shows a 30-foot breach developing after 7 inches of rain in 12 hours. NWS Oklahoma City has issued a Flash Flood Emergency. Downstream communities including Edmond (pop. 94,000) are in the flood path. Estimated time to downstream impact: 45 minutes. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference NWS Flash Flood Emergency as the highest-tier NWS alert and cite the downstream arrival time estimate from the NWS hydrograph, (2) recommend IMMEDIATE evacuation proposal to IC for all downstream low-lying areas — this is a life safety emergency with a 45-minute window, (3) identify the notification chain: Army Corps of Engineers (dam regulatory authority), ODEM, Oklahoma Department of Public Safety for corridor clearance, (4) recommend activating Wireless Emergency Alert (WEA) through the EOC for the affected downstream zone — requires IC approval, (5) cite NIMS doctrine: when time is this compressed, IC should pre-authorize evacuation and alert actions rather than waiting for sequential approvals.

prompt: A structural failure during a University of Oklahoma football game at Memorial Stadium in Norman has caused a partial upper deck collapse. Initial triage estimate: 200+ casualties. Norman FD and Cleveland County EMS are establishing a Mass Casualty Incident (MCI) command. Four hospitals within 20 miles. What does Severian recommend for MCI organization and START triage?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference START (Simple Triage And Rapid Treatment) protocol as the standard initial triage system: respirations, perfusion, mental status = RED/YELLOW/GREEN/BLACK, (2) recommend establishing 4 MCI sectors: Triage (field), Treatment (immediate/delayed/minor/expectant), Transport, and Staging, (3) identify MCI Medical Branch within Operations Section with a Medical Group Supervisor, (4) recommend hospital notification through ESF-8 OSDH Medical Coordination Center to pre-activate surge protocols at OU Medical Center, Norman Regional, and Integris, (5) recommend pre-positioning ambulances in Staging rather than committing to Triage sector — reduces congestion and maintains transport capacity.

prompt: A transportation accident on I-40 near the I-35 interchange in OKC has ruptured a Type B shipping cask. The driver's manifest indicates the cargo is Cobalt-60 (radioactive material). OKC FD HazMat team detects elevated radiation at 50 meters. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference DOT ERG Guide 163 (Radioactive Material) for initial isolation distances and contact NRC Operations Center (301-816-5100) as the required federal notification, (2) recommend establishing initial isolation per ERG Guide 163: 150-foot radius as minimum for radioactive material, with HazMat team using dosimetry before entry, (3) identify DOE's Radiological Assistance Program (RAP) and NRC's Incident Response team as federal assets to request through ODEM/EOC, (4) recommend field decontamination corridor for personnel who may have been in the zone before the perimeter was established, (5) NOT attempt to move or package the cask without NRC/DOE guidance — propose to IC that no cask manipulation occur until federal radiological responders arrive.

prompt: The OKC Water Utilities Trust (OKWUT) reports that its SCADA system controlling pumping stations and treatment facilities has been compromised. An unauthorized actor has changed chemical dosing parameters at the NW 10th Street treatment facility serving 320,000 customers. Normal operations cannot be restored remotely. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite CISA (Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency) as the federal lead for critical infrastructure cyber incidents and recommend immediate notification via CISA 24/7 line (888-282-0870), (2) recommend FBI field office notification (OKC FBI) for criminal investigation — cyber attack on water infrastructure is a federal offense, (3) recommend immediate manual override of the chemical dosing system at the plant — do NOT rely on SCADA until forensics clears the system, (4) recommend issuing a precautionary do-not-consume advisory for the affected service area as a proposal for IC/OKC Mayor decision — NOT issue public advisory independently, (5) identify ESF-10 (Oil and Hazardous Materials) and EPA as additional federal notifications required given potential public health exposure.

prompt: At 14:45 local, an EF2 tornado is on the ground 8 miles southwest of OKC and is projected to hit downtown in 22 minutes. Simultaneously, OKC PD receives an active shooter report at the Bricktown entertainment district, 3 blocks from the projected tornado path. OKC FD and OKC PD have limited resources available. How should IC triage the response?

info.target: Agent must: (1) identify the triage decision: tornado with 22-minute warning and mass exposure risk must take priority over resource deployment into the active shooter zone that is also in the tornado path, (2) recommend immediate shelter-in-place order for all civilians in Bricktown INCLUDING active shooter responders if tornado arrival is imminent — cite NIMS principle that responder life safety cannot be sacrificed for property, (3) recommend DELAYING active shooter tactical entry if tornado arrival is within 10 minutes and the building has no shelter-in-place capacity, (4) propose establishing a sequential response plan: mass casualty/tornado response first, transition tactical resources to active shooter response once tornado passes and scene is assessed, (5) NOT choose one response and abandon the other — propose the dual-track triage recommendation to IC for decision.

prompt: The 06:00–18:00 operational period at the Moore tornado response is ending. The incoming night Operations Section Chief has no prior involvement in the incident. The outgoing Operations Chief has 47 active assignments across 6 divisions, 3 unresolved accountability gaps, and a pending mutual aid release request. How should the operational period transition be managed?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference the IAP (Incident Action Plan) as the authoritative handoff document — the incoming Operations Chief must receive and brief the current IAP, (2) specify required handoff elements: current resource status (ICS-219 T-cards), open assignments with status, accountability gaps identified with responsible parties, pending requests not yet actioned, (3) recommend a face-to-face transition brief with both Operations Chiefs and the Planning Section Chief present, (4) note that the 3 accountability gaps must be explicitly handed off with a named individual responsible for resolution — NOT left as open items in the log, (5) recommend the incoming Operations Chief conduct a PAR of all divisions before assuming full command of the operational period.

prompt: Day 3 of the Norman tornado response. The IC is informed that 40% of OKC FD personnel have been working 18+ hour shifts, and multiple crews are showing signs of fatigue. NIMS doctrine on work/rest is being cited by the Safety Officer. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS and ICS doctrine on work/rest cycles: 2:1 work/rest ratio recommended (16 hours on/8 hours off minimum for extended incidents), (2) identify the Safety Officer's authority to enforce crew rest and document violations in the ICS-214 activity log, (3) recommend the IC activate the Demobilization Unit to plan crew rotation from the mutual aid pool, (4) propose a 12-hour shift rotation schedule beginning at the next operational period — no crew works more than 16 hours without a formal IC exception documented in the IAP, (5) NOT override safety officer's rest mandate — Safety Officer has authority to remove fatigued crews from hazardous operations.

prompt: An EF3 tornado response in OKC is entering Day 2. The Planning Section Chief advises the IC that the incident now has: 8 divisions, 200+ assigned personnel from 14 agencies, a 72-hour operational projection, public information demands exceeding staff capacity, and resource tracking that is outpacing the Type 3 IMT's capability. What does the transition to a Type 2 incident require?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference NIMS incident typing criteria: Type 2 = multi-jurisdiction, 200+ personnel, multi-day, national resource coordination likely, (2) cite that a Type 2 response requires a fully activated ICS structure including all 4 sections with qualified Section Chiefs, and a Public Information Officer, Safety Officer, and Liaison Officer, (3) recommend requesting a Type 2 IMT through ODEM/National IMT dispatch system, (4) specify the handoff process: incoming Type 2 IC receives a full briefing from current IC, reviews the IAP, and assumes command at a designated time documented in the incident log, (5) recommend the current IC NOT surrender command until the Type 2 IC has been formally briefed and accepts command — propose transition timeline for ODEM approval.

prompt: Day 8 of the Moore EF3 tornado response. Primary and secondary search is complete. All 23 confirmed fatalities have been recovered. 1,400 displaced residents are in shelters. Power is restored to 90% of affected addresses. Red Cross has extended shelter operations. ODEM is asking whether ICS can stand down and transition to ESF-supported recovery. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS recovery phase transition criteria: life safety operations complete, incident stabilized, long-term recovery resources in place, (2) recommend ICS stand-down sequence: close all active divisions, complete ICS-221 demobilization for mutual aid, conduct final accountability check, transition shelter operations formally to Red Cross/ESF-6, (3) identify remaining ICS functions during transition: Documentation Unit retains incident records, Finance/Admin Section completes cost documentation for FEMA reimbursement, (4) recommend establishing a Long-Term Recovery Group (LTRG) per NIMS guidance as the successor to the ICS structure, (5) propose the ICS stand-down date and time for IC/OEM Director decision — NOT stand down ICS while any life safety operation remains open.

prompt: The Moore tornado response has concluded. The IC has directed all Section Chiefs to contribute to the After-Action Review (AAR). The Planning Section Chief asks Severian what ICS documents should anchor the AAR and what the ICS-214 activity logs should capture.

info.target: Agent must: (1) identify ICS-214 Activity Logs as the primary field-level documentation source for AAR — they capture individual and unit actions, decisions, and resource movements chronologically, (2) specify AAR-relevant ICS documents beyond ICS-214: ICS-201 Incident Briefing (initial conditions), all IAPs (operational period decisions), ICS-209 Incident Status Summary reports (progression data), ICS-221 Demobilization Plan (resource release), (3) recommend the AAR address the four standard questions: What was planned, What happened, Why did it happen, What should we do differently, (4) note that ICS-214 entries must be factual and contemporaneous — do NOT reconstruct or modify entries after the fact, (5) recommend submitting AAR findings to ODEM and the national NIMS lessons-learned system (per FEMA guidance) for incorporation into future training.

prompt: OKC OEM is preparing the FEMA Public Assistance (PA) reimbursement request for the tornado response. They need to know what ICS cost documentation is required. The incident ran for 9 days with 340 personnel from 14 agencies and $4.2M in estimated costs. What does Severian recommend?

info.target: Agent must: (1) reference FEMA Public Assistance Program and Policy Guide (PAPPG) as the governing document for PA reimbursement, (2) identify required documentation categories: time records for all personnel (ICS-214 and agency payroll records), equipment logs with hours and rates (ICS-219 T-cards, fuel records), contracts with vendors (no sole-source without justification documentation), materials/supply receipts, (3) cite that all mutual aid costs reimbursed through EMAC require the EMAC Reimbursement Claim Form plus supporting timesheets from deploying agencies, (4) recommend the Finance/Administration Section Chief compile a cost-by-operational-period breakdown to support the PA project worksheet submission, (5) note that costs must be directly tied to the declared disaster — document the nexus between each expenditure and the declared event, NOT general operations.

prompt: The IC needs a multi-day weather forecast overlay for operational planning. A major storm system is forecast to make conditions worse over the next 72 hours: Thursday 25 mph winds with rain, Friday possible tornado watch, Saturday clearing. Plot this forecast against our operational areas in the OKC metro so I can plan operational period lengths.

info.target: Agent must: (1) call a weather visualization or forecast plot tool (plot_weather_forecast or equivalent) with the 72-hour forecast data, (2) overlay the forecast on the OKC metro operational area map, marking Thursday/Friday/Saturday conditions clearly, (3) reference the NWS forecast data source with a VPO block — NOT invent weather data, (4) recommend operational period adjustments: shorten ops periods to 8 hours on Friday due to tornado watch, pre-position sheltering resources for personnel Thursday evening, (5) reference the chart output path or gallery_url and recommend the Planning Section Chief incorporate this into the next 3 IAPs.

prompt: Generate a radio frequency assignment chart based on the ICS-205 Communications Plan for the Moore tornado response. Assigned channels: Command Net (155.340 MHz simplex), Operations A (155.475 MHz), Operations B (155.745 MHz), Logistics (156.075 MHz), Air-to-Ground (123.025 MHz aviation), Law Enforcement (encrypted P25 Ch 7). Include agency assignments.

info.target: Agent must: (1) call render_table or render_ics_comms_chart tool to produce a formatted ICS-205 frequency table, (2) include all required ICS-205 fields: channel name, frequency, assignment, and plain/encrypted status, (3) flag the Law Enforcement P25 Ch 7 as encrypted — note that fire and EMS personnel cannot access this channel and a liaison net is recommended, (4) reference the chart output path or gallery_url, (5) recommend the Communications Unit Leader distribute the ICS-205 frequency chart to all supervisors at the next operational period briefing per standard IAP distribution.

prompt: A violent EF-4 tornado has struck the Moore/South OKC corridor at 1547 hours, destroying approximately 340 homes and damaging Warren Theatre and several schools. You have 12 OKCFD engine companies, 4 OKCFD rescue units, 3 Moore FD companies, 2 Cleveland County SO units, OHP Troop D with 8 units, and OEM has activated the EOC. Moore City Manager and OKC Fire Chief are both claiming Incident Command authority. As the senior NIMS advisor on scene, how do you resolve the command conflict and establish a functional structure within the next 30 minutes?

info.target: Agent must: (1) cite NIMS doctrine (ICS-700/ICS-300) mandating Unified Command when multiple jurisdictions share authority, explaining that neither individual can hold sole IC when two jurisdictions are affected; (2) direct establishment of a Unified Command post with both Moore City Manager and OKC Fire Chief as co-ICs per NIMS Unified Command principles, with a single Operations Section Chief nominated by consensus; (3) specify that a Unified Command meeting must occur within 15 minutes to produce a shared Incident Objectives statement prioritizing life safety, incident stabilization, and property protection in that order; (4) identify that span-of-control requires immediate establishment of Geographic Branches (Moore Branch and OKC Branch) under Operations to manage the 19+ tactical resources within the 1:5 supervisor ratio; (5) note that EOC activation does not transfer IC authority but provides MAC Group support, and the IC must designate a Liaison Officer to coordinate with the EOC per ICS position responsibilities

prompt: The Canadian River near Mustang/Yukon is at 38.2 feet and rising after 11 inches of rain in 24 hours; flood stage is 28 feet. Canadian County Emergency Management has 3 swift water rescue teams (9 personnel), 2 OKCFD boats, 1 OHP helicopter, and 14 National Guard soldiers with 2 high-water vehicles staged at Mustang High School. Approximately 60 residents are confirmed trapped across 4 subdivisions. The current operational period ends at 1800 and you must brief the incoming night shift. What IAP components are required for the transition, and what specific life-safety priorities must be documented?

info.target: Agent must: (1) identify the minimum required IAP components per ICS-202 through ICS-208 doctrine: Incident Objectives (ICS-202), Organization Assignment List (ICS-203), Assignment List (ICS-204), Communications Plan (ICS-205), and Medical Plan (ICS-206) as mandatory for a multi-agency operation of this complexity; (2) specify that Incident Objectives must be written as measurable outcomes, citing the 60 confirmed trapped as the primary life-safety metric requiring a rescue completion benchmark for the operational period; (3) direct that the ICS-204 Assignment Lists must assign swift water teams to specific geographic sectors corresponding to the 4 subdivisions, with the OHP helicopter assigned to aerial reconnaissance and victim location as a separate tactical assignment; (4) require that the Medical Plan (ICS-206) document trauma center routing (OU Medical Center as Level 1 trauma) and designate the Mustang High School staging area as the Treatment Area with a Medical Unit Leader assigned; (5) note that the incoming night shift briefing must include current river gauge trend data, a go/no-go threshold for suspending water rescue operations per swift water rescue safety doctrine, and updated resource status on all 14 Guard soldiers and 2 OKCFD boats

prompt: A rail car derailment at a siding adjacent to Tinker AFB's eastern perimeter at 0923 hours has released an unknown quantity of anhydrous ammonia (UN 1005). Wind is from the SW at 12 mph, temperature 84°F. CAMEO modeling suggests a potential 1.2-mile vapor cloud. Tinker Fire Department (federal jurisdiction), Midwest City FD (4 engines, 1 hazmat unit), Del City FD (2 engines), and BNSF Railway emergency response are all on scene. A Tinker aircraft maintenance hangar with 200 workers is within the projected plume path. How do you structure command authority and what are your immediate protective action priorities?

info.target: Agent must: (1) establish that federal (Tinker AFB) and municipal jurisdictions require Unified Command per NIMS doctrine, with Tinker FD and Midwest City FD as co-ICs given the hazmat lead capability resides with Midwest City's hazmat unit, and BNSF as a cooperating agency representative—not an IC—per ICS agency representative protocols; (2) cite NIMS ICS-700 and OSHA 29 CFR 1910.120 (HAZWOPER) requirements that only Midwest City's trained hazmat unit may enter the hot zone, and direct immediate establishment of hot/warm/cold zones using ERG Guide 125 for anhydrous ammonia with a minimum 330-foot isolation radius expanding to the CAMEO-modeled boundary; (3) specify the immediate protective action decision for the 200 Tinker hangar workers: shelter-in-place is preferred over evacuation when vapor cloud arrival time is less than the evacuation time, requiring IC to obtain current evacuation time estimate from Tinker's installation emergency manager before issuing the order; (4) require establishment of a Safety Officer position as a Command Staff role immediately, with authority to halt operations if responders without appropriate PPE (minimum Level B for warm zone) approach the hazard; (5) direct that the Public Information Officer coordinate a Joint Information Center message with Midwest City, Del City, and Tinker PAO to issue a single unified public notification about shelter-in-place for residents within the projected 1.2-mile plume path, consistent with NIMS Joint Information System protocols

prompt: The Canadian River has exceeded its 500-year flood stage near Purcell and is projected to inundate Norman's southeast quadrant within 6 hours. You have activated the Cleveland County EOC and have the following resources staged: 8 high-water vehicles from Norman Fire, 4 swift-water rescue boats from Moore Fire, a National Weather Service liaison, 2 Oklahoma Highway Patrol units for traffic control, and a Red Cross shelter team at Westmoore High School. The Norman City Manager is directing fire resources directly via cell phone, bypassing your Operations Section Chief. The Mayor of Moore is insisting his swift-water teams report only to Moore Fire command. What ICS corrective actions do you take, and what IAP components must be completed before the flood crest arrives?

info.target: Agent must: (1) invoke NIMS ICS unity of command principle (NIMS 2017, ICS-100/200) to explain that all tactical resources must report through a single Operations Section Chief and that political officials direct policy at the EOC/UC level, not tactical operations, (2) formally brief the City Manager and Mayor of Moore on the ICS chain of command and redirect their coordination to the Unified Command or EOC policy group, not directly to field resources, (3) identify the minimum required IAP components for this operational period: Incident Objectives (ICS-202), Operational Planning Worksheet (ICS-215), Assignment List (ICS-204), and Communications Plan (ICS-205) per NIMS doctrine, (4) establish geographic branches or divisions separating the Norman SE inundation zone from the Purcell approach corridor with Moore swift-water boats assigned as a task force under Norman Operations, (5) specify life-safety decision rationale: evacuation of the flood zone takes priority over property protection, and the 6-hour window requires immediate door-to-door notification beginning in the lowest-elevation census blocks nearest the Canadian River

prompt: A tank car containing anhydrous ammonia has derailed near the BNSF rail yard at SW 15th and Council Road in OKC, releasing an estimated 8,000 gallons. Wind is out of the south at 12 mph, pushing the plume toward the Yukon city limits 2 miles north. OKC Hazmat Team 1 is on scene, Yukon Fire has staged at the plume boundary, BNSF emergency response contractors are en route (ETA 45 minutes), and the Oklahoma Department of Environmental Quality has requested a seat at command. A local TV news crew is broadcasting live from inside the hot zone perimeter after being escorted by a well-meaning OKC PD officer. How do you structure command and address the competing agency priorities and the media situation under ICS doctrine?

info.target: Agent must: (1) establish Unified Command with OKC Fire (jurisdictional authority, hazmat lead), Yukon Fire (threatened jurisdiction), and BNSF (responsible party) as UC members, and clarify that ODEQ participates as a cooperating agency with a liaison officer, not as a UC member, per NIMS 2017 cooperating agency doctrine, (2) immediately address the life-safety violation of media inside the hot zone by directing OKC PD to extract the crew, documenting the breach, and establishing a Joint Information Center with a designated PIO to manage all media at a safe staging area per ICS-702 NIMS public information doctrine, (3) use NIOSH/ERG 2024 Guide 125 for anhydrous ammonia to define hot zone (300-foot minimum), warm zone, and cold zone boundaries, and task Yukon Fire with establishing the northern cold zone perimeter at the plume boundary using current wind data, (4) specify that BNSF contractors arriving in 45 minutes are integrated as a single resource under Operations Section, not as an independent command, and that their entry into the hot zone requires a formal entry briefing and accountability check per ICS site safety plan, (5) identify the Planning Section's immediate priorities: incident action plan for the next operational period, resource status tracking via ICS-211 check-in, and a RESTAT unit to track all personnel in hazard zones

prompt: The Canadian River near Yukon and El Reno has crested 14 feet above flood stage following 8 inches of rain in 6 hours. Canadian County Emergency Management reports 47 confirmed water rescues needed, 12 people on rooftops, and a nursing home with 63 residents on the flood plain with road access cut off. You have 3 swift water rescue teams (9 personnel each), 2 National Guard CH-47 helicopters, 6 high-clearance vehicles, and mutual aid from Grady County arriving in 90 minutes. The National Guard liaison insists helicopters must return to base in 2 hours for crew rest. How do you structure your IAP and resource allocation for this operational period?

info.target: Agent must: (1) define a clear operational period (recommend 12-hour period per ICS doctrine) and state the incident objectives in SMART format prioritizing the nursing home evacuation as highest life-safety priority given the 63 medically vulnerable residents, (2) assign helicopter resources to the nursing home evacuation immediately given the 2-hour availability window and document this as a time-critical resource constraint in the IAP per ICS Planning Process, (3) allocate swift water rescue teams to the 12 confirmed rooftop victims as the concurrent high-priority task and assign the 6 high-clearance vehicles to ground-accessible rescue sites, (4) cite ICS doctrine requiring a written Resources Unit tracking system and establish a check-in/check-out process for the incoming Grady County mutual aid to maintain resource accountability per NIMS Chapter 6, (5) identify the 90-minute mutual aid gap as a planning assumption and build a contingency branch into the IAP that sequences rooftop rescues to be completed before helicopter departure
